sprite::NodePtr Struct Reference

A reference-counting pointer for Node objects. More...

#include <pointer.hpp>

 Gets the pointer value, after skipping any FWD nodes.

 Dereferences the pointer, after skipping any FWD nodes.

 Calls a member through the pointer, after skipping any FWD nodes.
NodePtr & operator[] (size_t i) const
 Dereferences a node pointer and then indexes the node.
NodePtr & operator= (Node &)
 Enables assignment from a Node (simplifies some parts of the implementation).

Detailed Description

A reference-counting pointer for Node objects.

This is based on boost::intrusive_ptr, so the reference count is stored within the Node object itself (to maximize performance). All dereferencing operations skip over FWD nodes, which are nodes that simply forward their reference to a new target. Those may occur when a node with multiple external references is rewritten.

Definition at line 23 of file pointer.hpp.

Node& sprite::NodePtr::operator * (   )  const [inline]
 

Dereferences the pointer, after skipping any FWD nodes.

Precondition: the pointer is not NULL.

NodePtr & sprite::NodePtr::operator[] (  size_t  i  )  const [inline]
 

Dereferences a node pointer and then indexes the node.

This function provides a simplified syntax for indexing.

Precondition: the point is not NULL, and the index is in-bounds.
